header.subtitle: LEADAI | EU AI ACT
header.title: AI Legal Standing
header.description: Capture key operator roles and risk classification before starting the assessment.

intro.title: Before we start your legal standing check
intro.subtitle: Please share a few details so we can personalise your results and follow up with a summary if needed.

share.label: Share the survey
share.title: LeadAI - EU AI Act Legal Standing
share.text: Take the LeadAI EU AI Act Legal Standing survey.
share.emailBody:
You can take the EU AI Act Legal Standing survey here:

{url}

form.name: Name
form.disclaimer: We'll only use this information in connection with this assessment.

survey.title: EU AI Act Legal Standing
survey.description: Answer the questions below to determine your operator role(s) and risk classification under Regulation (EU) 2024/1689.

validation.warning: Please answer all questions before generating your assessment.

buttons.generateAndGoNext: Generate Assessment and Go Next Step

summary.title: Assessment Summary
summary.empty: Submit the questionnaire to see your operator role and risk classification.

provider.label: Did your organization develop an AI system (or have one developed) with the intent to place it on the market or put it into service under your own name or trademark?
provider.purpose: Purpose: Determine if you are the Provider.
provider.explanation: If you build it (or commission it) and sell/use it under your brand, you are the Provider and carry the main compliance obligations.

deployer.label: Is your organization using an AI system to help run your business or perform professional tasks?
deployer.purpose: Purpose: Determine if you are the Deployer (user).
deployer.explanation: If you use an AI system in operations or professional work, you are a Deployer and must ensure proper use, monitoring, and records.

importer.label: Are you established in the EU and placing an AI system on the Union market that bears the trademark of a person established outside the EU?
importer.purpose: Purpose: Identify if you are the Importer.
importer.explanation: If you bring a non-EU provider's AI system into the EU market under that provider's trademark, you are the Importer.

distributor.label: Are you making an AI system available on the Union market in the supply chain (other than the provider or importer) without affecting its properties?
distributor.purpose: Purpose: Identify if you are the Distributor.
distributor.explanation: If you resell or make the AI system available without modifying it, you are a Distributor and must check CE marking and documentation.

authorized_representative.label: Have you received a written mandate from a non-EU provider to act on their behalf regarding specific tasks under this Regulation?
authorized_representative.explanation: If you are formally appointed by a non-EU provider to handle regulatory tasks, you are their Authorized Representative.

product_manufacturer.label: Are you a manufacturer placing a product on the market (e.g., a smart car or medical device) that incorporates an AI system developed by someone else?
product_manufacturer.purpose: Purpose: Identify a product manufacturer integrating AI.
product_manufacturer.explanation: If your product includes a third-party AI system, you may assume provider-like responsibilities for compliance.

non_eu_rep_appointed.label: If you are a non-EU provider, have you formally appointed an EU-based representative to handle regulatory inquiries?
non_eu_rep_appointed.purpose: Purpose: Check if a non-EU provider has an EU representative.
non_eu_rep_appointed.explanation: Non-EU providers must appoint an EU-based representative to handle regulatory obligations and inquiries.

distributor_access.label: Do you buy, sell, or provide access to an AI system that is already available within the EU?
distributor_access.purpose: Purpose: Confirm Distributor activity within the EU.
distributor_access.explanation: If you sell or provide access to an AI system already on the EU market, you must verify CE marking and documentation.

importer_non_original.label: Are you someone other than the original developer (Provider) or the entity that first imported it into the EU (Importer)?
importer_non_original.purpose: Purpose: Determine if you are not the Provider or Importer.
importer_non_original.explanation: If you are neither the original developer nor the first importer, you are likely a Distributor or Deployer with specific checks.

provide_as_is.label: Do you provide the AI system to users exactly as you received it, without modifying its intended purpose, instructions, or performance?
provide_as_is.purpose: Purpose: Confirm Distributor status by non-modification.
provide_as_is.explanation: If you supply the system without changes, you remain a Distributor. Modifying it can make you a Provider.

substantial_modifier.label: Are you making a significant change to an AI system that is already on the market or in use (e.g., changes to its purpose, functionality, or performance)?
substantial_modifier.explanation: Significant changes to purpose or performance can transfer Provider responsibilities to you.

in_scope_ai.label: Does the system operate with varying levels of autonomy and exhibit adaptiveness after deployment (meaning it isn't just a rigid, rule-based 'if-then' script)?
in_scope_ai.purpose: Purpose: Determine if the system is in scope of the AI Act.
in_scope_ai.explanation: If the system shows autonomy/adaptiveness beyond fixed rules, it likely qualifies as an AI system under the Act.

prohibited_practices.label: Does the system use subliminal techniques to distort behavior, exploit vulnerabilities (age/disability), or perform social scoring or untargeted scraping of facial images?
prohibited_practices.purpose: Purpose: Check for prohibited AI practices.
prohibited_practices.explanation: If yes, the system is prohibited and cannot be placed on the market or put into service.

safety_component.label: Is the AI system a safety component of a product that requires third-party conformity assessment (e.g., medical devices, machinery, toys)?
safety_component.explanation: Safety components typically fall into high-risk scope and require stricter conformity assessment.

annex_iii_sensitive.label: Is the AI system used in sensitive areas such as biometrics, critical infrastructure, education, employment, law enforcement, migration/asylum, or justice?
annex_iii_sensitive.purpose: Purpose: Detect Annex III high-risk use cases.
annex_iii_sensitive.explanation: If used in Annex III areas, the system is generally classified as high risk.

narrow_procedural.label: Does the system only perform narrow procedural tasks or improve human activity without making or influencing decisions?
narrow_procedural.purpose: Purpose: Check for narrow procedural exclusions.
narrow_procedural.explanation: If the system only assists with narrow procedural tasks, it may be excluded from high-risk classification.

profiling.label: Does the system perform profiling of natural persons?
profiling.purpose: Purpose: Check profiling impact on risk classification.
profiling.explanation: Profiling can trigger high-risk conditions and may override narrow procedural exclusions.

header.subtitle: LEADAI | ENTITY PROFILE
header.title: Entity Landing Page
header.description: Capture your entity profile information for compliance assessment.

coreIdentifiers.title: 1. Core Entity Identifiers
coreIdentifiers.description: These are the foundational details used for the Declaration of Conformity and legal filings.
coreIdentifiers.companyRegistrationNumberPlaceholder: e.g., VAT ID, LEI, or local Commercial Register number

operationalScope.title: 2. Operational Scope & Footprint
operationalScope.description: This helps determine which regional laws apply.
operationalScope.regionsOfOperationHint: Select all countries where the AI system is being placed on the market or put into service.
operationalScope.sectorHint: Specific sectors (like Finance or Critical Infrastructure) automatically trigger "High-Risk" status under the EU AI Act Annex III. You may select multiple.

companySize.title: 3. Company Size & Classification
companySize.description: The EU AI Act provides "SME (Small and Medium Enterprise) relief," so your size dictates your compliance costs and timelines.
companySize.marketRoleHint: Your role under the EU AI Act will be determined in the next step.

compliancePersonnel.title: 4. Key Compliance Personnel
compliancePersonnel.description: Auditors need a "Human-in-the-Loop" contact for the Evidence Vault.

authorizedRepresentative.title: Authorized Representative
authorizedRepresentative.description: The legal contact person residing in the EU (mandatory for non-EU companies).

aiComplianceOfficer.title: AI Compliance Officer / DPO
aiComplianceOfficer.description: The internal lead for the AI Management System (ISO 42001).

executiveSponsor.title: Executive Sponsor
executiveSponsor.description: The C-level leader accountable for AI risk (NIST AI RMF requirement).

t0.title: Purpose & Alignment
t0.blue: AI initiatives are tied to defined business outcomes and approved KPIs.
t0.orange: AI initiatives are launched without clear purpose or success criteria.

t1.title: Governance of Use Cases
t1.blue: Clear criteria exist for what should and should not be automated.
t1.orange: Automation decisions are made ad hoc with no risk/benefit analysis.

t2.title: Performance Review
t2.blue: AI objectives are reviewed in management meetings with KPI tracking.
t2.orange: AI projects operate outside performance review cycles.

t0.title: Roles & Ownership
t0.blue: Each AI system has a named owner and documented responsibility.
t0.orange: When AI issues arise, accountability is unclear.

t1.title: Leadership Behaviour
t1.blue: Leaders fund and model responsible AI decisions.
t1.orange: Leadership treats AI as a tech experiment without oversight.

t2.title: Escalation & Reporting
t2.blue: There is a defined path to report AI concerns or incidents.
t2.orange: AI incidents are handled informally or ignored.

t0.title: Policy & Integration
t0.blue: A documented AI Management System integrates ISO and legal requirements.
t0.orange: No formal AI policy exists or it is not followed.

t1.title: Auditing & Evidence
t1.blue: Internal audits and evidence logs are maintained for AI systems.
t1.orange: There is no audit trail for AI activities.

t2.title: Compliance Alignment
t2.blue: Controls map to ISO and EU AI Act articles.
t2.orange: Compliance activities for AI are fragmented or reactive.

t0.title: Assessment Process
t0.blue: AI risk and impact assessments are performed before and after deployment.
t0.orange: Risk assessments are skipped or done once then forgotten.

t1.title: Risk Coverage
t1.blue: Risks include individual, societal, and ethical impacts.
t1.orange: Only technical risks (like accuracy) are considered.

t2.title: Monitoring & Review
t2.blue: Post-market monitoring detects model drift and harm.
t2.orange: There is no system to monitor AI impacts after release.

t0.title: Provenance & Rights
t0.blue: Data sources, rights and provenance are documented and verifiable.
t0.orange: Datasets of unknown origin or licensing are used.

t1.title: Quality & Bias
t1.blue: Data is tested for bias and quality throughout the lifecycle.
t1.orange: Bias and quality issues go undetected until failures occur.

t2.title: Retention & Deletion
t2.blue: Data retention and deletion follow policy and law.
t2.orange: Data is kept indefinitely without control.

t0.title: User Disclosure
t0.blue: Users are informed when interacting with AI and understand its limits.
t0.orange: Users are not told when AI is used or its limitations.

t1.title: Documentation & Traceability
t1.blue: Technical and user documentation is complete and up-to-date.

t2.title: Logging & Explainability
t2.blue: Event logs support traceability and explainability.
t2.orange: Logs are incomplete or unavailable for investigation.

t0.title: Human-in-the-Loop
t0.blue: Humans review AI outputs before critical decisions.

t1.title: Training & Competence
t1.blue: Teams receive training on AI risk, ethics and oversight.
t1.orange: No formal training on AI oversight exists.

t2.title: Diversity of Expertise
t2.orange: AI projects run by a single technical team without input.

t0.title: Security Controls
t0.blue: Security testing covers AI-specific threats (poisoning, leaks).
t0.orange: AI systems use generic IT controls without AI threat testing.

t1.title: Privacy Management
t1.blue: PII handling complies with data-protection roles and laws.
t1.orange: AI processes personal data without defined responsibility.

t2.title: Incident Response
t2.blue: Security and privacy incidents are reported and analysed.
t2.orange: AI security breaches are under-reported or ignored.

t0.title: Process Control
t0.blue: The AI lifecycle is documented (design to deploy to retire).
t0.orange: Models are released without defined lifecycle control.

t1.title: Rollback & Change Mgmt
t1.blue: Tested rollback and change procedures exist.
t1.orange: No rollback plans; changes made directly in production.

t2.title: Performance Tracking
t2.blue: KPIs and drift metrics monitored in production.
t2.orange: Model performance is not tracked post-launch.

t0.title: Supplier Governance
t0.blue: Supplier AI responsibilities are defined in contracts.
t0.orange: Vendors provide AI without clear accountability.

t1.title: Customer Transparency
t1.blue: Customers receive AI documentation and safe-use guidance.
t1.orange: Customers are not informed about AI functions or limits.

t2.title: Feedback & Improvement
t2.blue: External feedback feeds into AI risk and improvement cycles.
t2.orange: Customer issues are ignored or handled off-record.
